name: Chang'e 6
first: First sample return from the lunar far side
description: Combines the architectures of Chang'e 4 (far-side communications) and Chang'e 5 (sample return). The Queqiao-2 relay satellite was launched first (March 2024) to support the mission. Chang'e 6 then collected 1.935 kg from Apollo Crater on the far side — material with very different composition from any lunar sample previously returned. The Moon's two hemispheres are radically different (the far side has thicker crust, fewer mare); these samples may finally explain why.

events.label: TOUCHDOWN
events.note: Lander descends to Apollo Crater (far side) 2024-06-02. All comms via Queqiao-2 relay.

events.label: ASCENT
events.note: Ascent vehicle lifts off; rendezvous with orbiter.

events.label: EARTH RETURN
events.note: Capsule lands in Inner Mongolia 2024-06-25 with the first far-side samples ever recovered.
